examples.title: Waterfall Model Phase Execution
examples.description: Demonstrates the sequential execution of SDLC phases in Waterfall model
examples.code:
PROCEDURE WaterfallModel()
BEGIN
 REQUIREMENTS:
 gatherUserRequirements()
 createSRS()
 validateRequirements()
 IF requirements_complete THEN
 PROCEED TO DESIGN
 ELSE
 REPEAT REQUIREMENTS
 ENDIF
 
 DESIGN:
 createSystemArchitecture()
 designModules()
 createDesignDocuments()
 IF design_approved THEN
 PROCEED TO IMPLEMENTATION
 ELSE
 REPEAT DESIGN
 ENDIF
 
 IMPLEMENTATION:
 codeModules()
 performUnitTesting()
 IF coding_complete THEN
 PROCEED TO TESTING
 ELSE
 REPEAT IMPLEMENTATION
 ENDIF
 
 TESTING:
 performIntegrationTesting()
 performSystemTesting()
 performAcceptanceTesting()
 IF all_tests_pass THEN
 PROCEED TO DEPLOYMENT
 ELSE
 RETURN TO IMPLEMENTATION
 ENDIF
 
 DEPLOYMENT:
 installInProduction()
 userTraining()
 
 MAINTENANCE:
 WHILE system_active:
 handleBugs()
 makeEnhancements()
 adaptToChanges()
 ENDWHILE
END
examples.explanation: Each phase must be completed and validated before proceeding to the next. If issues are found, the process may require going back to the appropriate previous phase.

examples.title: Iterative Development Loop
examples.description: Demonstrates iterative approach used in Agile/Spiral models
examples.code:
PROCEDURE IterativeDevelopment()
BEGIN
 iterations = 0
 max_iterations = DEFAULT_MAX
 
 WHILE iterations < max_iterations AND NOT complete:
 iterations = iterations + 1
 
 // Planning Phase
 planIteration(objectives)
 
 // Risk Analysis (for Spiral)
 identifyRisks()
 evaluateRisks()
 developMitigationStrategies()
 
 // Development Phase
 developFeatures()
 
 // Testing Phase
 testIncrement()
 
 // Evaluation Phase
 evaluateResults()
 gatherFeedback()
 
 IF stakeholder_approved THEN
 complete = TRUE
 ENDIF
 
 // Adjust for next iteration
 updateBacklog()
 reprioritizeFeatures()
 ENDWHILE
 
 IF complete THEN
 finalDeployment()
 ELSE
 handleIncompleteProject()
 ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: This loop represents iterative models where each cycle produces a potentially shippable product increment. Feedback from each iteration informs the next.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of iterations

examples.title: Requirements Traceability Matrix
examples.description: Algorithm for tracking requirements through SDLC phases
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CreateTraceabilityMatrix(requirements)
BEGIN
 // Initialize matrix
 CREATE matrix[requirements_count][phases_count]
 
 FOR each requirement IN requirements:
 matrix[req.id][REQUIREMENTS] = requirement.status
 
 // Trace through design phase
 matrix[req.id][DESIGN] = mapToDesignElement(req.id)
 
 // Trace through implementation
 matrix[req.id][IMPLEMENTATION] = mapToModule(req.id)
 
 // Trace through testing
 matrix[req.id][TESTING] = mapToTestCase(req.id)
 
 // Verify all requirements are covered
 IF matrix[req.id][TESTING] IS NULL THEN
 flagAsUntested(req.id)
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 
 // Generate coverage report
 coverage = calculateCoverage(matrix)
 
 RETURN matrix, coverage
END

// Forward tracing
FUNCTION mapToDesignElement(req_id)
BEGIN
 FOR each design_element IN design_documents:
 IF design_element.satisfies(req_id) THEN
 RETURN design_element.id
 ENDIF
 RETURN NULL
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm tracks each requirement from initial specification through design, implementation, and testing to ensure complete coverage and traceability.
